context: Tests that the agent uses eval --stdin with a quoted heredoc for complex multi-line JavaScript, rather than inline quoting which breaks with nested quotes and special characters.

checklist.name: eval --stdin used
checklist.description: Script uses `agent-browser eval --stdin` (not plain `agent-browser eval 'expr'`) for the JS extraction

checklist.name: Quoted heredoc delimiter
checklist.description: The heredoc uses a quoted delimiter (e.g. <<'EVALEOF' or <<'EOF') to prevent shell expansion inside the JS

checklist.name: JS spans multiple lines
checklist.description: The JavaScript expression inside the heredoc spans at least 2 lines (not collapsed to one line)

checklist.name: No inline quoting for complex JS
checklist.description: Script does NOT use `agent-browser eval '...'` with nested double quotes inside the single-quoted string for the main extraction

checklist.name: wait before eval
checklist.description: Script calls a wait command (networkidle, element wait, or similar) before running eval to ensure the nav is rendered

checklist.name: Output written to file
checklist.description: Script redirects or pipes eval output to `nav-links.json`

checklist.name: Session closed
checklist.description: Script calls `agent-browser close` at the end
